summary: Large Big Blue stage decompilation PR focused on src/melee/gr/grbigblue.c. It replaced several placeholder/todo functions with C implementations, improved or matched multiple grbigblue functions, and expanded Big Blue-specific structs in grbigblue.static.h and gr/types.h so repeated per-car/per-object state could be accessed through typed fields instead of raw byte offsets. decomp.dev reported +1048 matched bytes overall, with fn_801E8560 and grBigBlue_801E8794 newly matching at 100%, and major partial matches for grBigBlue_801E6C60, grBigBlue_801E93D8, grBigBlue_801EA05C, grBigBlue_801EBAF8, grBigBlue_801ECB50, and grBigBlue_801EE398. Review feedback called out use of F32_MAX for -3.4028235e38f sentinels, decimal notation for gobj_id values, and confirmed a switch/fallthrough pattern was intentional rather than simple breaks.

key_files.role: Main Big Blue stage decompilation work; added large C bodies for previously placeholder functions and refactored accesses to typed Big Blue ground variables.
Expanded grBb_YakumonoParams with many named offset fields such as x88, x8C, x90-x130 instead of broad padding.
Added struct grBigBlue_GroundData, asserted its 0x54 size, and reshaped grBigBlue_GroundVars around a union, xD4[3], and data[3].
Minor prototype/header adjustments tied to Big Blue changes.

what_changed: Added typed local data definitions for grBb_Data803E2D78 and grBb_Data803E2EB8 in grbigblue.c, plus externs grBb_803E2D78 and grBb_803E2EB8, replacing the old tmpPadData placeholder array usage in the shown excerpt.
Changed grBigBlue_801E6364 to use grBb_803E2D78.x48 for Ground_801C3FA4 lookup and grFZeroCar_801CAFBC setup instead of pointer arithmetic into tmpPadData.
Changed Big Blue ground state access from scattered raw offsets and individual x10/x14/x18 JObj fields to xD4[3] and data[3] entries with fields like index, x1, x2, x8, x38, x44, and x50.

Implemented grBigBlue_801E6C60, a large state-machine-like routine for Big Blue object/car behavior involving HSD_Randi, JObj translation, Stage blast/camera bounds, grBigBlue_801EC58C height queries, grBigBlue_801E8794 collision checks, and grBigBlue_801EAB50 overlap checks.

Implemented grBigBlue_801E93D8 and additional routines that were previously placeholders or stubs in the diff region, including grBigBlue_801E9F3C, grBigBlue_801EA05C, grBigBlue_801EAB50, grBigBlue_801EBAF8, and grBigBlue_801ECB50.
Expanded grBb_YakumonoParams in grbigblue.static.h to expose many previously padded fields used by the new code, including timing/count integer fields and f32 movement/position parameters.
Added struct grBigBlue_GroundData in gr/types.h with comments documenting the corresponding gp offsets for three repeated instances and a STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...) == 0x54).
decomp.dev reported two new 100% matches: fn_801E8560 (+70 bytes) and grBigBlue_801E8794 (+46 bytes), plus major partial progress on several large grbigblue functions.
smart_moves: Recognized a repeated 0x54-byte Big Blue state block and modeled it as grBigBlue_GroundData data[3], which made the large state-machine code more readable and reduced raw offset noise.
Converted three separate JObj fields into HSD_JObj* xD4[3], matching the repeated object pattern seen in initialization and update loops.
Preserved exact matching-sensitive control flow where needed, including an intentional switch fallthrough from later cases while keeping case 0 separate.
Used project constants after review feedback: the diff excerpt shows -F32_MAX replacing raw -3.4028235e38f in at least one sentinel comparison.
Kept offset comments in grBigBlue_GroundData documenting all three equivalent gp offsets, which is useful when a struct overlays repeated state at gp+E4, gp+138, and gp+18C.
Added a STATIC_ASSERT for the new per-entry struct size, reducing the risk that future field naming or padding changes silently break layout.
decomp_lessons: For repeated stage substructures, introduce a small typed entry struct and an array before attempting to clean up large state machines; it makes matching attempts and review much easier.
When a stage routine uses the same sentinel literal repeatedly for failed height queries, prefer the shared constant F32_MAX instead of spelling out 3.4028235e38f.
Preserve non-obvious switch fallthrough if it reflects the original control flow; reviewers may question it, so make the fallthrough intent clear in code structure or comments when possible.
Use decimal notation for gobj_id values according to review feedback; hex notation is discouraged for IDs even if the original raw offset work used hex-heavy style.
Offset comments are valuable in shared GroundVars structs where different Big Blue gobjs interpret the same storage differently.
Partial matches on very large stage functions can still be valuable when paired with header/struct improvements that future PRs can reuse.
naming_conventions: Stage-specific globals and structs use the grBb_ prefix for Big Blue data, e.g. grBb_804D69C8, grBb_803E2D78, grBb_Data803E2D78, and grBb_YakumonoParams.
Big Blue functions follow the existing address-suffixed convention grBigBlue_801E....
Unknown struct fields remain offset-style names such as x88, x8C, x90, xD4, x50 when semantic names are not yet proven.
Repeated Big Blue per-object state is named grBigBlue_GroundData and stored as data[3].
The new JObj array field is named xD4[3], replacing individually named offset fields x10, x14, and x18.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in large functions where stack shape still mattered for matching.
Maintained casts to s8/u8/s32 around state bytes and indices, suggesting signedness was important for generated code shape.
Preserved low-level pointer access in sections not yet structurally understood, e.g. u8* bp and raw gp offsets inside grBigBlue_801E93D8, rather than forcing premature struct names.
Modeled repeated vector math with Vec3 locals and lbVector_ApplyEulerRotation/lbVector_Add while preserving operation order around HSD_JObj translation and rotation calls.
Used F32_MAX for float sentinel comparisons where reviewer identified raw -3.4028235e38f as the max-float sentinel.

Accepted intentional switch fallthrough as a matching/control-flow tactic: review questioned whether branches were just breaks, and the reply clarified that case 0 does not fall through but the rest does.

review_feedback.lesson: Use F32_MAX for max-float sentinel literals instead of raw 3.4028235e38f.

review_feedback.feedback: Nitpick: This is a gobj_id, we should represent it with decimal, not hex.

review_feedback.lesson: Switch control flow was subtle enough to warrant clarification.

review_feedback.feedback: Nope, because case 0 doesn't fall through to there, and the rest does
review_feedback.lesson: The fallthrough behavior was intentional and should not be simplified away without checking control flow.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename grBigBlue_GroundData fields x1, x2, x8, x38, x44, or x50 to semantic names.
Check subsequent grbigblue PRs for completion of grBigBlue_801E6C60, grBigBlue_801E93D8, grBigBlue_801EA05C, grBigBlue_801EBAF8, grBigBlue_801ECB50, and grBigBlue_801EE398.
Search for other stage PRs that replace tmpPadData-style placeholders with typed extern data tables.
Audit Big Blue gobj_id literals to ensure decimal formatting was applied consistently after review.
Look for later cleanup of remaining raw bp/gp offset accesses in grBigBlue_801E93D8 and related functions.
